event: Left lower leg cactus plant injury
Minor penetrating injury to his left lower leg from a cactus plant
Presentation to Emergency Department via ambulance
Heart Rate 125 beats per minute, sinus tachycardia
Admitted to the Intensive Care Unit
Piperacillin-Tazobactam IV 4.5g stat and then ceased
Emergency left lower limb fasciotomy, debridement and below knee amputation
High suspicion of necrotizing fasciitis with an LRINEC Score of 5
Extensive left foot and below knee tissue necrosis
“Dishwasher fluid” appearance along with liquefied fat
Large areas of unviable skin and muscle
Background history of Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis
Background history of mild Ulcerative Colitis
Postoperatively, patient was transferred to ICU intubated
Left internal jugular vascath insertion in preparation for continuous renal replacement therapy
Initial blood cultures isolated Group B Streptococcus pneumoniae
Ventilation sedation: Propofol 170 mg/hr, Fentanyl 40 mcg/hr
Intravenous Immunoglobulin G therapy 100 g initiated
Taken back to theatre to confirm source control and viability of the left stump
Developed new onset of rapid atrial fibrillation with HR 110 bpm
Echocardiography: Moderate segmental left ventricular dysfunction, Ejection Fraction 40-45%
Continuous renal replacement therapy with Oxiris filter applied
Developed features consistent with Sepsis Induced Coagulopathy (ISTH-SIC criteria)
Wound culture showed Group B Streptococcus pneumoniae
Patient developed new onset purpura over the right forearm
Multiple weeping skin tears on right upper thigh
Ongoing rapid atrial fibrillation HR 130 bpm
Patient remained oliguric despite being challenged with Furosemide IV 250 mg
Developed fulminant hepatic failure and encephalopathy
Severely deconditioned and developed ICU acquired weakness
Unarousable and had no response to noxious stimuli
Remained intubated and ventilated on Pressure Support Ventilation
Remained deeply sedated despite being off sedation
New onset lateralizing signs to the left upon painful stimuli compared to right side
CT Brain: No acute intracranial pathology
Significant deterioration despite ongoing medical therapy
Patient commenced on palliative care pathway
